rationale: Neither candidate surfaces the actual ground-truth concern (yenuo26's question about whether reduce_scatter needs deeper verification beyond hasattr in test_expert_parallel_layout.py), so recall is 0 for both. X gives a clean, well-grounded correctness verification (DPMetadata construction, init_vllm_model_parallel_group semantics, cleanup/destroy paths) but surfaces only one soft, non-blocking nit, yielding high precision but low actionability. Y surfaces five distinct, evidence-backed, concrete findings (untested guard branches, untested pre-hook integration, dead-code None-check, docstring/collective-invariant gaps) with specific suggested fixes, giving stronger actionability, though the entire review_text block is redundantly repeated three times verbatim, which is a real presentation flaw that shouldn't be rewarded as added value.
